expectations.text: Output recommends a named tier (Minimal, Standard, or Strict) — not just a list of tools
expectations.evidence: No tier system. Items labeled 'High Priority', 'Medium Priority', 'Low Priority' but no named tier.

expectations.text: Tier is Standard (not Minimal, not Strict) — solo with paying customers and contractors incoming warrants Standard
Output includes a .pre-commit-config.yaml snippet with at least black and ruff
expectations.evidence: Has black (rev 24.4.2) and ruff (v0.4.4) in the pre-commit config

expectations.text: Output includes a GitHub Actions CI workflow snippet
expectations.evidence: Full ci.yml with Postgres service — actually more Django-specific than the with_skill version

expectations.text: Output includes a PR template or explains how to create one
Output includes effort estimates (e.g. '~5 min') for at least half the items
expectations.evidence: No effort estimates. Uses 'do this today' and priority labels instead.

expectations.text: Output explains WHY each major item matters (not just what it is)
expectations.evidence: Most items have brief why context (e.g. 'This is the single most important thing. It prevents accidental pushes directly to main and broken deployments.')

expectations.text: Output ends with or includes order-of-operations guidance (pre-commit before CI before security)
expectations.evidence: 'Recommended Order of Operations' section present, though orders branch protection before pre-commit

notes: No tier system is the main gap. Effort estimates absent. The CI snippet is actually better (includes Postgres service for Django) and adds GitHub Environments gate — useful additions not in the skill.
